tweets:
My form inputs were sometimes controlled, sometimes uncontrolled. React kept warning me. I had no idea what it meant.

Here's what I learned about the control model in React (thread):

1/ Controlled = parent owns the value

You pass value + onChange. The input is a pure reflection of parent state. Every keystroke flows up, parent re-renders, value flows down.

const [name, setName] = useState('');
<input value={name} onChange={e => setName(e.target.value)} />

3/ The React warning

'A component is changing an uncontrolled input to be controlled.'

Happens when value switches from undefined → string. Fix: value={value ?? ''} — never pass undefined.

4/ When to use which

Controlled: live validation, transform input, sync with other fields.
Uncontrolled: simple form, only need value on submit, performance (large forms).

5/ Design for both

Support either mode. If value !== undefined → controlled. Else → uncontrolled. Never mix in one instance. Document it.

Full post: link in bio.
